# citrusinvest

> Open-source Rust backtest + strategy engine: the **yuzu** backtest core and the **lemon**
> strategy DSL. Strategies are authored in lemon (a small text DSL) and lowered to a JSON `Expr`
> tree that the engine evaluates into an equity curve, trades, and metrics. Pure and I/O-free;
> compiles to native and WASM.

## Docs

- [Lemon language reference](docs/lemon.md): complete syntax — lexical elements, operators and
  precedence, grammar (incl. `let`), the full op reference, worked examples, and gotchas.
- [Lemon prompt card](docs/lemon-prompt.md): a compact, prompt-ready cheat sheet for generating lemon.
- [Engine architecture](docs/backtest-engine.md): panels, NAV model, metrics, the `Report` JSON contract.
- [Input data layout](docs/data-layout.md): bring-your-own prices/fundamentals/panels tree, series names, PIT notes.
- [Contributing & release flow](CONTRIBUTING.md).

## API (docs.rs)

- [yuzu-core](https://docs.rs/yuzu-core) — `run_backtest(spec_json, ctx, price_key, cfg) -> Report`.
- [lemon-lang](https://docs.rs/lemon-lang) — `parse(src) -> Value`, `format(&value) -> String`.
- [yuzu-data](https://docs.rs/yuzu-data) · [yuzu-source-s3](https://docs.rs/yuzu-source-s3)

## Machine-readable (for tool-use / structured output)

- [Lemon op catalog (JSON)](schema/op-catalog.json): every op, its arguments, types, and defaults.
- [Lemon spec JSON Schema](schema/lemon-spec.schema.json): schema for the tagged `Expr` tree.

## Examples

- [Basic backtest](crates/yuzu-core/examples/basic_backtest.rs): panel → lemon → `run_backtest` → metrics.
